segments.text: Then the LORD appeared to Abraham by the Oaks of Mamre in the heat of the day, while he was sitting at the entrance of his tent.
And Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. When he saw them, he ran from the entrance of his tent to meet them and bowed low to the ground.
My lord,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: said Abraham,
if I have found favor in your sight, please do not pass your servant by.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Let a little water be brought, that you may wash your feet and rest yourselves under the tree.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: And I will bring a bit of bread so that you may refresh yourselves. This is why you have passed your servant’s way. After that, you may continue on your way.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Yes,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied,
you may do as you have said.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So Abraham hurried into the tent and said to Sarah,
Quick! Prepare three seahs of fine flour, knead it, and bake some bread.
segments.speaker: Abraham hurried into the tent and

segments.text: Meanwhile, Abraham ran to the herd, selected a tender and choice calf, and gave it to a servant, who hurried to prepare it.
Then Abraham brought curds and milk and the calf that had been prepared, and he set them before the men and stood by them under the tree as they ate.
Where is your wife Sarah?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
There, in the tent,
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: he replied.
Then the LORD said,
I will surely return to you at this time next year, and your wife Sarah will have a son!
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Now Sarah was behind him, listening at the entrance to the tent.
And Abraham and Sarah were already old and well along in years; Sarah had passed the age of childbearing.
So she laughed to herself, saying,
After I am worn out and my master is old, will I now have this pleasure?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: And the LORD asked Abraham,
Why did Sarah laugh and say, ‘Can I really bear a child when I am old?’
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Is anything too difficult for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you—in about a year—and Sarah will have a son.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: But Sarah was afraid, so she denied it and said,
I did not laugh.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: No,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: replied the LORD,
but you did laugh.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: When the men got up to leave, they looked out over Sodom, and Abraham walked along with them to see them off.
And the LORD said,
Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do?
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Abraham will surely become a great and powerful nation, and through him all the nations of the earth will be blessed.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: For I have chosen him, so that he will command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing what is right and just, in order that the LORD may bring upon Abraham what He has promised.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then the LORD said,
The outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is great. Because their sin is so grievous,
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: I will go down to see if their actions fully justify the outcry that has reached Me. If not, I will find out.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: And the two men turned away and went toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing before the LORD.
Abraham stepped forward and said,
Will You really sweep away the righteous with the wicked?
segments.speaker: Abraham stepped forward and

segments.text: What if there are fifty righteous ones in the city? Will You really sweep it away and not spare the place for the sake of the fifty righteous ones who are there?
segments.speaker: Abraham stepped forward and

segments.text: Far be it from You to do such a thing—to kill the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous and the wicked are treated alike. Far be it from You! Will not the Judge of all the earth do what is right?
segments.speaker: Abraham stepped forward and

segments.text: So the LORD replied,
If I find fifty righteous ones within the city of Sodom, on their account I will spare the whole place.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text: Then Abraham answered,
Now that I have ventured to speak to the Lord—though I am but dust and ashes—
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: suppose the fifty righteous ones lack five. Will You destroy the whole city for the lack of five?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: He replied,
If I find forty-five there, I will not destroy it.
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: Once again Abraham spoke to the LORD,
Suppose forty are found there?
segments.speaker: Once again Abraham

segments.text: He answered,
On account of the forty, I will not do it.
segments.speaker: Once again Abraham

segments.text: Then Abraham said,
May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak further. Suppose thirty are found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: He replied,
If I find thirty there, I will not do it.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: And Abraham said,
Now that I have ventured to speak to the Lord, suppose twenty are found there?
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: He answered,
On account of the twenty, I will not destroy it.
segments.speaker: Abraham

segments.text: Finally, Abraham said,
May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak once more. Suppose ten are found there?
segments.speaker: Finally, Abraham

segments.text: And He answered,
On account of the ten, I will not destroy it.
segments.speaker: Finally, Abraham

segments.text: When the LORD had finished speaking with Abraham, He departed, and Abraham returned home.
